name: Cascada del Molino de la Chorrera Trail
name_original: Cascada del molino de La Chorrera desde Tragacete
description: Hike an easy 3.0-mile trail in Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park to the picturesque 30-meter Molino Waterfall in 1 hour and 23 minutes.
region_intro: Hiking around Tragacete offers access to the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, a region characterized by extensive pine forests, verdant valleys, and varied mountain ranges. The landscape features significant hills, unique karst formations, and the sources of major rivers like the Júcar and Cuervo. Hikers can explore dramatic gorges and areas where rivers cascade over moss-covered limestone. This diverse terrain provides a range of experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Tragacete The most popular hiking route is Cascada del Molino de la Chorrera Trail, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This easy path leads to a picturesque waterfall within the natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is From Tragacete to Cerro S. Felipe — Serranía de Cuenca, a difficult 12.1 miles (19.5 km) path. This route offers a challenging ascent to a prominent hill with significant elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Molino Waterfall – Mill Waterfall loop from Tragacete, a 1.9 miles (3.1 km) trail leading through forested areas, often completed in about 53 minutes. Hiking around Tragacete is defined by vast pine forests, unique karst formations, and the sources of major rivers. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding treks. The routes in Tragacete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Tragacete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Tragacete?
A: Tragacete is nestled in the heart of the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, offering a diverse array of landscapes. You'll encounter extensive pine forests, verdant valleys, and impressive mountain ranges with significant hills like Mogorrita and San Felipe. The region is also known for its unique karst formations, including dramatic gorges (hoces), caves, and sinkholes, as well as spectacular waterfalls formed by rivers like the Júcar and Cuervo.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Tragacete?
A: Yes, Tragacete offers several easy routes perfect for families and beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013332001/cascada-del-molino-de-la-chorrera-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Molino de la Chorrera Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.9 km path leading to a picturesque waterfall. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38145845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Waterfall – Mill Waterfall loop from Tragacete&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter 3.1 km trail through forested areas.

Q: Where can I find impressive waterfalls or river sources on a hike near Tragacete?
A: The region is famous for its beautiful waterfalls and river sources. You can visit the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuervo River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt; Natural Monument, known for its cascades over moss-covered limestone. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is also a popular spot, accessible via an easy trail from Tragacete. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the River Tagus&lt;/a&gt; is another significant natural feature in the area.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers around Tragacete?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364741681/from-tragacete-to-cerro-s-felipe-serrania-de-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Tragacete to Cerro S. Felipe — Serranía de Cuenca&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 19.5 km path with significant elevation gain, leading to a prominent hill. Another moderate option that offers a good workout is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Felipe Hill – Puntal del Cerro San Felipe loop from Albergue Juvenil de San Blas&lt;/a&gt;, covering 11.6 km.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Tragacete?
A: The hiking routes around Tragacete are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 out of 5 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from vast pine forests to unique karst formations, and the well-maintained paths that lead to spectacular natural monuments like waterfalls and river sources. Over 500 hikers have used komoot to explore this area.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes starting near Tragacete?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38145845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Waterfall – Mill Waterfall loop from Tragacete&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 3.1 km easy loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Blas Youth Hostel – Fuente de Blas loop from Albergue Juvenil de San Blas&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.1 km route that starts from the Albergue Juvenil de San Blas, a common starting point for local excursions.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park?
A: The Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. While hiking, you might spot various local fauna, including deer, roe deer, and wild boar. The skies are home to numerous raptors such as griffon vultures and golden eagles. If you visit in autumn, you might even experience the 'berrea,' the spectacular deer rutting season.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Beyond the stunning waterfalls and river sources, hikers can seek out impressive viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/603722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Serranía de Cuenca&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the mountainous landscape. The area's unique karst formations, including dramatic gorges and the nearby 'Ciudad Encantada' (Enchanted City), also serve as natural landmarks to explore.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tragacete?
A: Tragacete offers picturesque scenery throughout the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. During the rainy season, the waterfalls, especially at the Source of the Júcar River, are particularly impressive. Autumn also brings the 'berrea' (deer rut), a spectacular natural event.

Q: Are there any places to stay or eat near the hiking trails in Tragacete?
A: Tragacete itself is a settlement where you can find amenities. The Albergue de San Blas (San Blas Youth Hostel) serves as a convenient starting point for several circular hiking routes and offers accommodation. The region also has various cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the local villages for refreshments after your hike.

Q: How many hiking routes are available around Tragacete?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes available around Tragacete, catering to various skill levels. This includes 4 easy routes, 5 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced trekkers.
tips: A few kilometers from the town of Tragacete and located in a wonderful setting on the bed of the Júcar river, the Molino waterfall is an impressive place that is worth visiting. You can do the short itinerary by parking in the recreational area, ideal for the whole family, or take a slightly longer tour along the various paths that lead here.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cascada del Molino de la Chorrera Trail?
faqs.answer: There are multiple spots to park your vehicle conveniently near the trailhead in Tragacete, Cuenca. This makes accessing the route straightforward.

faqs.question: Is the Cascada del Molino de la Chorrera Trail suitable for families and beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is considered easy and is very suitable for families with children and beginners. It offers a pleasant and accessible outdoor experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally an easy path, making for a comfortable hike. You'll walk through a pleasant natural environment leading to the waterfall.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The main highlight is the beautiful 30-meter high Molino Waterfall, which is noted as the first waterfall of the Júcar River. The area also offers pleasant natural scenery and viewpoints with spectacular views.

faqs.question: Is the Cascada del Molino de la Chorrera Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs are not detailed, trails within natural parks in Spain generally require dogs to be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. It's always best to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike here?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the Cascada del Molino de la Chorrera Trail itself. However, always respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the waterfall?
faqs.answer: The area is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking and the waterfall can be particularly impressive after periods of rain. Summer is also popular, but can be warmer.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The komoot tour suggests a duration of approximately 1 hour and 23 minutes to complete the 4.87 km route. This can vary based on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the komoot route for the Cascada del Molino de la Chorrera Trail follows approximately 26% of an official OpenStreetMap trail also named "Cascada del molino de la Chorrera".

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Tragacete, a small village in Cuenca. You can find local amenities, including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in Tragacete to refresh yourself before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What makes this waterfall special compared to others in the region?
faqs.answer: The Cascada del Molino de la Chorrera is notable as the first waterfall of the Júcar River. It offers a tranquil and picturesque natural environment, often highlighted as a peaceful alternative to the more famous nearby Nacimiento del Río Cuervo.
